subject: Physics
title: Heisenberg's Big Idea
description: A short lesson to help you apply Heisenberg's Big Idea.

hook.text: Werner Heisenberg proved you can never know everything about a particle at once. Nature forbids it.

buildup.text: In 1927, Heisenberg showed that measuring a particle's position precisely blurs its momentum.

discovery.text: This isn't about bad instruments. The universe genuinely doesn't have both values defined at once.

twist.text: Heisenberg was only 25 when he discovered this. He developed it while recovering from hay fever.

climax.text: The uncertainty principle is a bedrock of quantum mechanics. No experiment has ever violated it.

punchline.text: Know where it is? Lose where it's going. Nature's trade-off.

quiz.question: What does Heisenberg's uncertainty principle state?
options: You cannot precisely know both position and momentum simultaneously
Particles can be in two places at once
